description: Recurring Tasks + Labels/Tags System + Right-Click Context Menu + Bulk Multi-Select Actions. This feature adds task metadata enhancements: recurring tasks that auto-regenerate on completion, colored labels/tags for tasks with filtering, a right-click context menu for quick actions, and checkbox-based bulk selection with bulk operations.
steps: STEP 1 - Extend the LocalTask interface in frontend/app/dashboard/tasks/page.tsx to include new fields: recurrence?: { type: 'daily' | 'weekly' | 'monthly' | 'custom'; interval?: number; days?: number[] } | null; labels?: { id: string; name: string; color: string }[]; These are stored in localStorage alongside existing task data.

STEP 2 - Create a new component at frontend/components/tasks/LabelManager.tsx that allows creating custom labels with name + color picker. Default labels: Work (#3b82f6 blue), Personal (#8b5cf6 purple), Urgent (#ef4444 red), Health (#10b981 green), Learning (#f59e0b amber). Store labels in localStorage key 'chronai-labels'. Export a useable hook or context for labels.

STEP 3 - Add label display on task cards (SortableTaskCard and ListRow in tasks/page.tsx): Show small colored dots or pills next to the task title. In the TaskDetailPanel, add a label selector section that shows available labels as clickable chips.

STEP 4 - Add a label filter bar above the task board/list: horizontal scrollable row of label chips. Clicking a label filters tasks to only show those with that label. An 'All' chip to clear filter. Store active filter in component state.

STEP 5 - Add recurrence UI to the TaskDetailPanel and Create Task Modal: a 'Repeat' dropdown with options: None, Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Custom. For Custom, show interval input and day-of-week checkboxes. Visual indicator on task cards: a small repeat/refresh icon next to recurring tasks.

STEP 6 - Implement recurring task auto-regeneration: In the handleToggleTask function, when a recurring task is marked as 'done', automatically create a new copy of the task with status 'todo', the same title/labels/priority, and an updated due date (calculated from the recurrence pattern). The completed original stays in 'done'.

STEP 7 - Create a right-click context menu component at frontend/components/tasks/TaskContextMenu.tsx: Uses a portal-based absolutely-positioned menu that appears on right-click. Menu items: Edit (opens detail panel), Delete, Duplicate, Move to... (submenu: To Do, In Progress, Done), Add Label (submenu with available labels), Set Priority (submenu: High, Medium, Low, None). Style: dark bg-[var(--surface)] border border-[var(--border)] rounded-xl shadow-2xl, items with hover states.

STEP 8 - Wire up the context menu to task cards: On SortableTaskCard and ListRow, add onContextMenu handler that prevents default and shows the context menu at cursor position. Implement each menu action (edit opens detail, delete shows confirmation, duplicate creates a copy with 'Copy of' prefix, move changes status, add label toggles label, set priority updates priority).

STEP 9 - Add bulk multi-select mode: Add a 'Select' toggle button in the tasks header. When active, show checkboxes on each task card/row. Selected tasks tracked in component state as Set<string>. Bulk action bar appears at bottom when 1+ tasks selected: Complete All, Delete All, Move to..., Set Priority, Add Label. Each bulk action applies to all selected tasks.
STEP 10 - Verify: Run npm run build and npm run lint to ensure everything compiles cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build succeeds with no errors
npm run lint shows no errors
LocalTask interface includes recurrence and labels fields
Labels can be created with custom names and colors
Default labels (Work, Personal, Urgent, Health, Learning) exist
Tasks display their labels as colored indicators
Label filter bar filters tasks by selected label
Recurrence can be set on tasks (daily/weekly/monthly/custom)
Recurring tasks show a visual indicator (repeat icon)
Completing a recurring task auto-creates the next occurrence
Right-click on any task shows a context menu with Edit/Delete/Duplicate/Move/Label/Priority
Bulk select mode shows checkboxes and a bulk action bar
Bulk actions (complete, delete, move, priority, label) work on selected tasks
